perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Tripath may in its sole discretion, immediately upon written notice to Distributor, make direct sales to certain accounts in the Territory ("Direct Sales Accounts"), including accounts to which Distributor has made sales.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Tripath may in its sole discretion, immediately upon written notice to Distributor, make direct sales to certain accounts in the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'including accounts to which Distributor has made sales,' there's a potential contradiction. While Tripath retains the right to direct sales, it now becomes ambiguous whether existing Distributor-acquired accounts are also subject to direct sales. This omission contradicts the initial understanding that the Distributor is the primary point of contact for all sales within the Territory, as highlighted in section 2.2. The new text is vague, which creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Distributor will use best efforts to develop the local market and promote the sales of Products within the Territory. Distributor will not sell outside the Territory without Tripath's prior written permission, which will not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.justification: The original text of section 5.1 explicitly included 'accounts to which Distributor has made sales' as potentially subject to direct sales by Tripath. The changed text removes this clarification, creating ambiguity. This omission contradicts section 2.2, which outlines the Distributor's role in developing the market and promoting sales within the Territory. By removing the explicit inclusion of the distributor's accounts, the modification introduces the possibility that Tripath can directly target accounts previously developed by the distributor, undermining the distributor's role outlined in 2.2 and creating a direct conflict in enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: 9.2 Distributor may request stock rotation return of slow moving Products, which will be authorized by Tripath provided that: (a) the Product is contained in Tripath's then current Distributor Price List, (b) the Product is packaged as it was originally shipped, and (c) a purchase order for equal value accompanies the request for return. Such stock rotation returns will not exceed five percent (5%) of the dollar value of Distributor's previous six (6) months net purchase orders from Tripath.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.2 Distributor may request stock rotation return of slow moving Products. Such stock rotation returns will not exceed five percent (5%) of the dollar value of Distributor's previous six (6) months net purchase orders from Tripath.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the original conditions for stock rotation returns, the new clause gives the impression that any slow-moving products can be returned at any time without consideration for whether Tripath still offers it, the condition of the product packaging or if it's accompanied by a purchase order for equal value. This creates an contradiction, because Tripath will still be at liberty of the distributor request of stock returns, but Tripath won't have the conditions that are being mentioned, since those were removed and no longer apply. The initial section 9.1 states that 'Distributor may return Products only after receiving written authorization from Tripath', this new change, doesn't imply that such returns will be authorized, thus creating uncertainty in enforcements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1 Distributor may return Products only after receiving written authorization from Tripath. All authorized returns will be shipped by Distributor freight prepaid. Unauthorized returns may be reshipped to Distributor, freight collect.
perturbation.justification: The original text outlines specific conditions under which the distributor can request stock rotation returns. The changed text removes these conditions, implying that returns can be made without them. This directly contradicts section 9.1, which states that 'Distributor may return Products only after receiving written authorization from Tripath.' By removing the conditions, it creates uncertainty as to when and how Tripath authorizes returns, weakening or negating the constraint imposed by section 9.1, as Tripath no longer has specific return conditions.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Reports and Audits 11.1 Distributor will submit reports as may be reasonably requested by Tripath, including, without limitation, monthly reports of inventory on hand and sales relating to the Products. Sales data will include, without limitation, Product part number, quantity, resale price, special pricing, distributor invoice number, and customer name. Distributor will submit these reports electronically on a timely basis each month and in no case later than two weeks after the beginning of the month.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Reports and Audits 11.1 Distributor will submit reports as may be reasonably requested by Tripath.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specifics of what these reports must contain (inventory, sales data, pricing, customer names, etc.) and the mandate of electronic submission within two weeks of each month's beginning, the change creates significant ambiguity. While reports are still required, the lack of detail contradicts the implicit understanding that Tripath needs structured, timely, and detailed data for effective oversight and planning. This contradicts Tripath's right to Audit Distributor's Records, from Section 11.2 since Tripath could not longer verify the correct sales and inventory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.2 Tripath may have an authorized Tripath representative, at Tripath's cost, audit Distributor's records relating to sales and inventories of Products, including, without limitation, records pertaining to any claims submitted by Distributor for price protection, stock rotation, returned Products, ship from stock and debit, DPA allowances, and credit requests. Upon prior written notice, Distributor will provide reasonable access to such records during normal business hours at Distributor's office. Distributor agrees to maintain all such records for a minimum of three years.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated specific details (inventory, sales data, pricing, customer names, etc.) in the reports, and electronic submission within two weeks of each month's beginning. This level of detail directly supports Tripath's ability to audit Distributor's records, as outlined in Section 11.2. The changed text removes these specifics, creating a contradiction because Tripath can no longer effectively audit the distributor's records related to sales and inventory, as the reports lack the necessary information. The change contradicts Tripath's explicit right to audit records related to sales and inventories of Products.
